evals.prompt: We need to understand how the billing null guard bug got introduced across the cloud and appwrite services
evals.expected_output: /fanout decomposes this into 3-5 independent research questions targeting billing/null handling across multiple repos, dispatches as parallel subagents in one message, and synthesises into a plan.
expectations: Decomposes into 3-5 research questions
Each question is narrow and factual (answerable by reading code, not requiring creative work)
Questions are independent (no question depends on another's answer)
At least one question targets the Cache/billing layer
At least one question targets the database/document layer
Dispatches subagents in a single Agent tool call message (not sequentially)
Does NOT start editing before all subagents return

evals.prompt: Rename the handleUser function to processUser in src/User.php
evals.expected_output: /fanout should NOT decompose this — it's a trivial single-file edit. The skill should tell the user to just edit directly.
expectations: Does NOT decompose into subagents
Recommends a direct edit instead
Response is short and actionable

evals.expected_output: Swoole audit runs 11 parallel subagents scanning the current directory for all categories: runtime hooks, blocking I/O, shared state, pools, long-running footguns, concurrency primitives, server config, signals, shared memory, extension compat, testing.
expectations: Identifies Swoole entry points (Swoole\Http\Server, Swoole\Server, etc.) with file:line
Dispatches 11 parallel subagents (one per audit category) in a single message
Produces a P0/P1/P2 prioritised report
Each finding includes file:line and a one-line description
Skips categories that don't apply and notes which were skipped
Excludes vendor/ and tests/ from scanning
Does not produce false positives for safe scratch targets (node_modules, dist, etc.)

evals.prompt:
/swoole-fix
  bin/http.php:18 — PDO before Co::set
  src/Service/Cache.php:24 — Redis static singleton across coroutines
evals.expected_output: Swoole fix classifies the 2 findings (1 mechanical, 1 structural), loads swoole-expert as primary reference, dispatches fix subagents, returns 2 diffs with swoole-expert section citations.
expectations: Classifies each finding as mechanical, structural, or uncertain
Loads the swoole-expert skill as primary reference for fix patterns
Dispatches fix subagents (one batch for mechanical, one per structural)
Each proposed diff cites a specific swoole-expert section
Does NOT auto-apply diffs — presents them for human review
Does not fix more than 10 findings in one run

evals.expected_output: Merge conflict resolution dispatches per-file analysis as parallel subagents with both sides' history, classifies each file as orthogonal/overlapping/contradictory, auto-resolves the first two, leaves contradictory files for review.
expectations: Enumerates conflicted files via git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U
For each file, gathers both sides' history (git log HEAD vs MERGE_HEAD) and the common ancestor
Classifies each file as orthogonal, overlapping, or contradictory
Auto-resolves orthogonal and overlapping files (writes resolved content + git add)
Does NOT auto-resolve contradictory files — lists them for human review with both sides' intent summaries
Does NOT run git commit — leaves that to the user
Does NOT delete a file if only one side deleted it — flags as contradictory

evals.expected_output: Lists only Swoole-related items across the marketplace: swoole-expert skill, /swoole-audit command, /swoole-fix command. Other plugins with no Swoole matches are omitted entirely.
expectations: Output is filtered to items matching 'swoole' in name or description
Includes swoole-expert skill from appwrite-conventions
Includes /swoole-audit command from appwrite-skills
Includes /swoole-fix command from appwrite-skills
Does NOT include unrelated items (e.g. /fanout, utopia-http-expert)
Groups results by plugin with plugin description headers
